title: Define WinlinkBackend trait — v0.5 prep Step 1 (Pat greenfield refactor)
description:
Operator decision 2026-05-18 (Codex + Cameron convergence): tuxlink moves off Pat to native Rust at v0.5 via staged replacement (not flag-day). Step 1 is the architectural boundary — define a WinlinkBackend trait that decouples tuxlink's UI/config layer from the Pat HTTP sidecar so future native + Pat implementations can co-exist behind one interface.

Trait surface (from current Pat usage in src-tauri/src/pat_process.rs + pat-client integration):
- list_messages(folder: Folder) -> Vec<MessageMeta>
- read_message(id: MessageId) -> Message
- send_message(msg: OutboundMessage) -> Result<MessageId>
- connect(transport: TransportConfig) -> Result<SessionHandle>
- disconnect(session: SessionHandle) -> Result<()>
- status() -> BackendStatus
- stream_log() -> impl Stream<Item = LogLine>

Two conceptual impls:
1. PatBackend — wraps current HTTP-to-Pat-sidecar. Lands in this issue's PR.
2. NativeBackend — placeholder/stub in this issue; fleshed out in Steps 3-10.

Discipline: this IS a hard-to-undo architectural decision (the trait shape constrains every future backend implementation), so build-robust-features applies — BUT TIGHTLY SCOPED per the operator's 2026-05-18 framing:
- Brainstorm: 1 hour max (concept only — error model, async vs sync, session-handle ownership, log-stream cancellation)
- Spec: trait definition + behavior contract + 5-10 test cases (NOT 24+)
- >=1 cross-provider adrev round (NOT 5 — Codex review on the trait surface only)
- Plan: trivial (trait file + stub NativeBackend + PatBackend wrapper around current pat-client code)
- Ship as one PR

Follow-up steps (each gets its own bd issue when this one merges):
2. Freeze Pat backend as reference (PatBackend implements the trait)
3. Build native mailbox + B2F parser/writer with golden fixtures from Pat / wl2k-go
4. Build B2F session state machine over generic async IO
5. Build CMS telnet client + fake CMS harness
6. Pat-vs-native parity tests over same fixtures
7. Operator-only live CMS smoke gate
8. AX.25/KISS layer (KISS = protocol compliance per memory feedback_ai_amateur_radio_reliability)
9. Native VARA as separate milestone (clean-room: reverse-engineering writeups read as inverse spec, NOT adoption guide)
10. Default native after parity+soak; remove Pat sidecar after one fallback release

Memories to read before starting:
- feedback_discipline_triage_rule (this IS architectural; pipeline applies, but tightly scoped)
- feedback_clean_sheet_concepts_only
- feedback_ai_amateur_radio_reliability
- project_v05_modem_design_posture
- project_g90_vara_standard_works_firsthand
- feedback_no_atomic_decisions_to_operator

Replaces nothing in v0.0.1 — this is purely v0.5 prep. v0.0.1 continues to ship the Pat sidecar via the cred-handling fork (PR-A merged + PR-B merged); the trait wraps that existing surface without disturbing the shipped wizard or PatProcess flow.

close_reason: Shipped via PR #67 (merged 2026-05-19T01:20Z). WinlinkBackend trait + PatBackend + NativeBackend stub + 18 tests landed on feat/v0.0.1.

title: Task 2 config implementation (AMD-1 nested schema + AMD-11 + validate_identity + write_config_atomic) — HARD prerequisite for wizard-cluster plan
description:
Surfaced by 2026-05-18 wizard-cluster plan-review R1 friction lens (P0-1, P0-3) and verified against shipped src-tauri/src/config.rs.

Current state of src-tauri/src/config.rs (verified 2026-05-18):
- HAS flat schema with: callsign, grid_square, pat_mbo_address, winlink_password_present, wizard_completed (all top-level)
- MISSING: nested connect/identity/privacy structs per AMD-1
- STILL HAS: winlink_password_present per AMD-11 should-be-dropped
- MISSING: validate_identity() function (per AMD-1 loose-validator semantics)
- MISSING: write_config_atomic() function (per Task 2 spec; used by wizard_persist_cms in wizard-cluster spec §3.2)

Gap pattern: AMD-1 (2026-05-17) was a plan-text amendment; the corresponding CODE update to config.rs never shipped. Same gap exists for AMD-11 (2026-05-18 field drop). The plan amendments document intent; code amendments implement it — they don't auto-cascade.

Scope:
1. Update Config struct to nested shape per AMD-1: connect{connect_to_cms, transport}, identity{callsign, identifier, grid}, privacy{gps_state, position_precision}, pat_mbo_address.
2. Drop winlink_password_present per AMD-11.
3. Implement validate_identity(s: &str) -> bool per AMD-1's loose-validator semantics (non-empty + no internal whitespace + ≤32 chars + ASCII-printable).
4. Implement write_config_atomic(config: &Config) -> Result<(), ConfigError> using tempfile + std::fs::rename to /tuxlink/config.json.
5. Update all existing call sites + tests in config_test.rs.
6. Pitfalls entry: document the 'plan amendment != code amendment' gap pattern (in implementation-pitfalls.md as a new entry; cross-cutting discipline).

Full build-robust-features pipeline per memory feedback_no_carveout_on_cross_provider_adrev — no upstream skip.

Blocks: tuxlink-ln3 wizard impl plan (which assumes this work shipped). Without this landing first, wizard impl plan Phase 0 will catch the gap AND Phase 3.2 wizard_persist_cms will fail to compile (calls undefined validate_identity + write_config_atomic).

Also flagged by R3 P0-2 (§5.13 schema version downgrade refusal — implemented in write_config_atomic's pre-write read-check).
notes: PR #66 opened 2026-05-18 (https://github.com/cameronzucker/tuxlink/pull/66) on bd-tuxlink-4mt/task-2-config-impl. 7 commits: Phase 0-7. 34/34 tests pass. close on merge via deliverable.
close_reason: merged via PRs #66/#68/#69/#64+#65/#57/#55/#58 earlier 2026-05-18/19; bd state was stale post-merge, cleaning up in willow-cypress-heron session-end batch

title: Task 3 (PatProcess) amendment — render Pat's non-secret config at spawn time
description:
HARD prerequisite for wizard-cluster impl (tuxlink-ln3 / Tasks 9/10/11/11.5). Surfaced by Codex R5 P1 on wizard-cluster spec adrev §7.3 item 4.

Pre-cred-handling: Pat's config.json was pre-existing or operator-managed; PatProcess just spawned the binary.
Post-cred-handling (PR-A merged): Pat's config.json no longer carries the WL2K password (deleted from cfg.SecureLoginPassword); Pat reads password from OS keyring. BUT Pat still expects its own config.json with non-secret fields (callsign, MBO address, transport selection).

Wizard now writes: (1) tuxlink config.json + (2) keyring entry. Wizard does NOT write Pat's config. Without something filling that gap, Pat spawns but has no callsign/MBO config = nonfunctional.

Scope: amend Task 3 (PatProcess in src-tauri/src/pat_process.rs) to render Pat's config.json from tuxlink's config + the keyring callsign convention, at Pat-spawn time. Likely involves: read tuxlink config; build Pat config struct/json; write to Pat's expected path (~/.config/pat/config.json); spawn Pat.

Full build-robust-features pipeline per memory feedback_no_carveout_on_cross_provider_adrev (no upstream skip). Pipeline: brainstorm (small; design is settled by cred-handling spec + AMD-13 + this gap) → spec → 5-round adrev → spec revision → plan-write → 4-round plan-review → revision → TDD impl.

Blocks: tuxlink-ln3 impl plan (wizard cluster) — without this amendment landing, the wizard impl would ship but Pat couldn't operate. Wizard impl plan should document this as a HARD prerequisite if not yet done.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-z5f-winlink-backend-trait/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-756-pat-config-render. Branch: bd-tuxlink-756/pat-config-render. Created 2026-05-19 01:22:40 UTC by badger-oak-dahlia.

title: Fork-setup: create tuxlink-pat repo, mirror upstream, document build + sync workflow
description:
Per ADR 0011 §'Implementation follow-ups (queued as bd tasks)'. Operational setup for the tuxlink-pat fork:

1. Operator-decided: repo home (personal account / new org / vendored subdirectory)
2. Create the repo + initial commit (la5nta/pat upstream at chosen point in time)
3. Set up branch protection (no force-push to main; PR-required for merges)
4. Document the build pipeline: how tuxlink's src-tauri/ build sources Pat from the fork (build-from-source vs prebuilt-binary-release vs git submodule)
5. Document the upstream-sync workflow (cadence, who runs it, conflict-resolution policy)
6. Update tuxlink's src-tauri/ build to source Pat from the fork instead of upstream binary download

Full build-robust-features pipeline per ADR 0011 §3 (brainstorm with Cameron for the operator-decision points, then 5-round cross-provider adrev, then writing-plans-enhanced, then plan-review-cycle, then impl).

Blocks: cred-handling refactor (needs fork to exist), v0.0.1 plan amendments (needs fork layout to document).
notes: PR-B opened: https://github.com/cameronzucker/tuxlink/pull/54. Closes via deliverable on merge.

title: Cred-handling refactor: Pat reads WL2K from OS keyring (first tuxlink-pat patch)
description:
Per ADR 0011 §2 'First refactor target.' First agentic patch against tuxlink-pat after fork-setup completes.

Scope:
- Pat reads WL2K password from OS keyring (secret-service on Linux Gnome/KDE, Keychain on macOS, CredentialManager on Windows) instead of from config.json
- config.json retains callsign + non-secret config; secrets move out entirely
- Tuxlink wizard (Task 9) writes the password into the keyring directly via Rust keyring crate; wizard never touches a config.json path containing the password
- Live-CMS smoke binary (Task 6) reads from keyring at run time; no env vars, no temp files, no disk persistence beyond keyring

Upstream PR contribution: pursue after fork-side patch ships. Secret-service / Keychain / CredentialManager are platform-native and Pat upstream may welcome the security improvement.

Full build-robust-features pipeline (brainstorm → 5-round cross-provider adrev → writing-plans-enhanced → plan-review-cycle → TDD impl → Codex on impl diff → PR).

Blocks: Task 6 resume (tuxlink-nk7), Task 9 wizard work (tuxlink-ko0).
notes: Phase 10 shipped 2026-05-18 by maple-magpie-oak. PR-B opened as cameronzucker/tuxlink#59 against feat/v0.0.1; bumps external/tuxlink-pat to PR-A merge SHA 4969aa86. Awaits Cameron's merge → bd close tuxlink-mib at that point. Worktree /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-mib-mib-cred-keyring/ is CLEAN; dispose per ADR 0009 post-merge. Plan LDC banner updated to 🚧 In progress.
close_reason: Shipped via PR-A (tuxlink-pat#2, merge SHA 4969aa86) + PR-B (tuxlink#59, merge SHA 81b5dc4) both merged 2026-05-18. Pat now reads WL2K credentials from OS keyring; web UI deleted (~20k LoC); 24 unit tests + 2 integration tests in credstore package + 7-test exchange callback regression. Worktree disposal + plan LDC final ✅ flip rolled into next planning cycle.

title: ADR 0011: Fork Pat for tuxlink (cred-handling + ongoing refactor)
description:
Architectural decision triggered 2026-05-18 during Task 6 brainstorm. Cameron rejected continuing to depend on upstream Pat as-is because Pat persists WL2K passwords to its config.json in plaintext, which is a non-negotiable defect for a production system. Bandaging the symptom at each tuxlink call site (env-var workarounds, temp-file mitigations) is bandaid spiraling.

Decision (Cameron, inline): FORK Pat as a tuxlink-owned variant. Refactor cred-handling to use OS keyring as the first patch. Continue refactoring other Pat limitations as discovered, using the full build-robust-features pipeline per refactor. Open to upstream contributions where the fork's changes are accepted upstream.

This ADR captures: context (the bandaid pattern), decision (fork), alternatives considered (continue-as-is / Rust rewrite / hybrid), consequences (Go codebase to maintain, fork-sync discipline, v0.0.1 scope expansion to include fork-setup + cred refactor + plan amendments for affected tasks 5/6/9), and the transition plan.

Light pipeline: decision is made; ADR is a write-up + Codex round + PR. Full build-robust-features pipeline NOT required for the ADR itself (it's documentation of a made decision). Subsequent tasks (fork-setup, cred-refactor, plan amendments) DO use the full pipeline.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-qiv-adr-0011-fork-pat. Branch: bd-tuxlink-qiv/adr-0011-fork-pat. Created 2026-05-18 10:44:47 UTC by oak-fjord-swallow.

title: Bug: get_tuxlink_sessions.py disagrees with block-main-checkout-race.sh on live-session detection
description:
Per towhee-wren-aspen's 2026-05-18 reviewer response (dev/incidents/2026-05-18-main-checkout-race-hook-loop-reviewer-response.md, reviewer Action 3) — the script and the hook read the same .git/session-leases/*.json files with the same 30-min default cutoff, but in the 2026-05-18 incident the script reported 'No live tuxlink sessions in this repo' while the hook was treating a 4-minutes-ago lease as live. They should agree by construction; they don't. This is a port-quality bug independent of the CLAUDE.md framing fix in PR #39 — even after the framing fix, a script that reports 'no live sessions' while the hook denies will continue to confuse future agents who consult it for legitimate informational purposes (e.g., 'is anyone else working in this repo right now?').

Reviewer's likely causes, in priority order:

1. **Timestamp parse failure (most likely).** The hook writes lastSeenUtc using bash's date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%6NZ (6-digit microseconds + trailing Z). Python's datetime.fromisoformat is stricter than people expect about fractional-second precision and trailing-Z handling. The script's parse_iso_utc returns None on parse failure, and on None the lease is silently skipped — causing under-reporting without any error message.

   Reproduce:
       ts=$(jq -r .lastSeenUtc .git/session-leases/<any-session-id>.json)
       python3 -c "
       import sys
       sys.path.insert(0, '.claude/scripts')
       from get_tuxlink_sessions import parse_iso_utc
       print(repr(parse_iso_utc('$ts')))
       "

   If None: that's the bug. Fix: align format on both sides (widen parse_iso_utc OR constrain the hook).

2. **Lease dir path mismatch.** The hook resolves the lease dir via git rev-parse --git-common-dir (= .git/session-leases/). The script resolves via Path(__file__).resolve().parent / '..' / '..' / '.claude' / 'session-leases'. If these resolve to different paths, the script reads an empty/stale dir and reports 'no sessions.'

   Reproduce: print both dirs from a test harness; compare.

3. **File-extension/glob mismatch.** Less likely. The script's sorted(lease_dir.glob('*.json')) should pick up every *.json file. If the hook is writing to a dir without .json extension, the script silently skips.

Priority: P1 (blocks future port-quality regressions of same shape; cheap fix; safety-stack relevance). Should land as its own atomic PR after diagnosis confirms which cause applies. No dependencies on PRs #37/#38/#39/#40.
notes: PR #44 opened 2026-05-18 by oak-fjord-swallow. Tasks 1-7 + 9-10 complete; Task 8 (PARITY-1 pitfalls entry) deferred until PR #43 (superseded PR #40 after rebase) merges and brings HOOK-1+LEASE-1 into feat/v0.0.1. Closes via deliverable on merge. Auto-memory feedback_stale_lease_means_worktree.md updated in parallel (out-of-repo).

title: Fix File → Quit native handling (post-PR-70 smoke gap)
description:
Smoke test of PR #70 (Task 7 menu bar) on 2026-05-19 surfaced one failure: clicking File → Quit (or pressing Ctrl+Q) does nothing. Root cause: wire_menu_events in src-tauri/src/menu.rs emits the menu:file:quit event to the 'menu' Tauri channel and stops. The React frontend (src/App.tsx — still Tauri-React boilerplate) has no listen('menu', ...) handler, so the event fires into dead air.

Every menu item has the same shape, but Quit is the one item users have a hard expectation about ('Quit must quit').

Fix: special-case Quit in wire_menu_events so it works natively without waiting for Tasks 9+ to wire the frontend listener. ~4 lines:

  if id == 'menu:file:quit' { app_for_handler.exit(0); }

The event still emits, so future frontend listeners (e.g., 'discard unsaved draft?' dialog in Task 14 Compose) can still observe + intercept later.

Discipline: plumbing-class per feedback_discipline_triage_rule — spec is this issue body, TDD-direct, no Codex round.
acceptance_criteria: File → Quit closes the app cleanly. Ctrl+Q closes the app cleanly. menu_test still passes (event manifest unchanged). cargo build --lib still warning-free. Operator-smoke-confirmed before merge.

description:

docs/plans/2026-05-18-cred-handling-plan.md' to flag AMENDMENT markers missing the bd-issue cite.

title: v0.0.1 plan: AMD-* amendments for Tasks 5/6/9/11 (post-fork; post-keyring)
description:
Per ADR 0011 §6 'Transition plan for v0.0.1.' Bundled spec amendments for affected tasks after fork + cred-refactor land.

Tasks affected:
- Task 5 (Pat HTTP client, SHIPPED): may need amendment if fork's cred refactor changes Pat startup behavior (e.g., keyring-prompt on first run)
- Task 6 (Live-CMS smoke, PAUSED): smoke binary uses keyring-backed cred lookup directly; no env vars, no temp files
- Task 9 (Wizard screen 1 — Winlink account, NOT STARTED): wizard writes WL2K password to OS keyring; no config-file persistence
- Task 11 (Wizard screen 3 — test send, NOT STARTED): test-send pulls password from keyring (not from config-file path)
- v0.0.1 plan §'Tools and Dependencies': add keyring Rust crate dep; document secret-service system-package requirement (Debian/Ubuntu AppImage target)

Single AMD-N callout bundle per the existing AMD-1..10 pattern.

Not a full build-robust-features pipeline (these are plan-text amendments, not new features). Standard writing-plans-enhanced is overkill here; the AMD amendment pattern from earlier PRs (#34/#35/#36) is the precedent.

Blocked by: fork-setup (need fork layout to document), cred-handling refactor (need actual API surface to document).

title: AppImage: document secret-service system-package dep (Debian/Ubuntu)
description:
Per ADR 0011 §6 'Transition plan for v0.0.1' (last bullet). The cred-handling refactor moves WL2K password to OS keyring; on Debian/Ubuntu (the AppImage target), keyring access requires the secret-service D-Bus interface backed by gnome-keyring-daemon (or kwalletd5 on KDE).

Scope:
- Document libsecret-1-0 / gnome-keyring / kwallet system-package requirement in install docs
- AppImage manifest: declare runtime dep if AppImage supports it; otherwise document in README install steps
- Test AppImage on a fresh Ubuntu LTS install to verify the dep is correctly named

title: Tauri webview shows TV-static / GPU artifacts on first launch (Pi 5, Wayland, webkit2gtk-4.1)
description:
Symptom: every time `pnpm tauri dev` (and presumably `pnpm tauri build` AppImage) launches the Tauri window on the dev Pi 5, the window appears as TV-static / uninitialized-GPU-memory graphical artifacts. Any click or window manipulation triggers a repaint and the page content appears normally.

This is an upstream webkit2gtk + Wayland + Mesa V3D (Pi 5 VideoCore VII GPU) class of bug — the WebKit GPU compositor allocates the webview surface before the first frame is painted, so the window shows whatever's in uninitialized GPU memory until an event forces a repaint.

NOT introduced by tuxlink code. Has been present since the Tauri scaffold was added (commit 52d4181, 2026-04-22). Just easier to notice now after Task 7's menu work made the operator stare at the window carefully.

Mitigation env vars to test (least invasive first):
- WEBKIT_DISABLE_DMABUF_RENDERER=1 — disables WebKit's DMA-BUF rendering path, known fix for several Wayland-render bugs
- WEBKIT_DISABLE_COMPOSITING_MODE=1 — disables GPU compositing entirely, software fallback
- GDK_BACKEND=x11 — falls back to XWayland; nuclear option

If any mitigation works, bake it into:
- The dev experience (env var in package.json scripts or .env.local equivalent)
- The release build (tauri.conf.json's runtime config or AppImage launcher script)

For Task 17 (AppImage packaging, tuxlink-cs7): the release artifact MUST ship with the working mitigation env var pre-set so end users don't see TV-static on their Pi/Linux desktops.

Acceptance:
- Operator verifies one of the env vars eliminates the TV-static symptom on their Pi 5
- Working env var is documented in development.md
- AppImage build (Task 17) bakes the env var into the launcher

Priority P3 (polish — doesn't block functionality; v0.0.1 ships with or without this fix per operator call).
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-wfw-fix-webkit-dmabuf. Branch: bd-tuxlink-wfw/fix-webkit-dmabuf. Created 2026-05-19 21:40:23 UTC by willow-cypress-heron.
close_reason: WEBKIT_DISABLE_DMABUF_RENDERER=1 set in-Rust at run() startup via PR #73; operator-verified clean launch, no regression

title: In-situ desktop mocks: tuxlink on Debian, Raspberry Pi OS, Ubuntu LTS
description: Follow-on to tuxlink-x4s (PR #56). The 4 existing mockup files show tuxlink as a floating native window on a black backdrop — accurate to convention but doesn't unambiguously communicate 'this is a desktop app' to first-time viewers. Add a new mockup HTML showing the same canonical tuxlink window (synthesis dock-on shape) in-situ on three Linux desktop environments: Debian (GNOME), Raspberry Pi OS (LXDE/Wayfire), Ubuntu LTS 24.04 (GNOME + Yaru). Each scene shows the DE's panel/dock/wallpaper around the tuxlink window. Pure CSS — no external images. Cosmetic show-off; no design decisions change.

title: Refactor mockup chrome from macOS to Linux/Adwaita
description: The 4 HTML mockups under docs/design/mockups/ accidentally rendered the app window with macOS traffic-light controls (red/yellow/green circles) and an Apple-leading font stack. Tuxlink is a Linux/Tauri app; the mocks should show GNOME/Adwaita-style chrome (subdued − □ × controls on the right, flat headerbar) to accurately reflect the project's end state. Cosmetic/vain — no design decisions change. Includes regenerating the 12 PNGs and a brief README note.

title: Add PARITY-1 pitfalls entry (follow-up to PR #44 / tuxlink-arv)
description:
Plan v2 Task 8 (PARITY-1 entry in Section 2 of docs/pitfalls/implementation-pitfalls.md) was deferred from PR #44 (tuxlink-arv) per Cameron 2026-05-18: 'separate small follow-up PR.' Picks up once both PR #43 (HOOK-1+LEASE-1) and PR #44 (arv script fix) merge to feat/v0.0.1.

Scope:
- Extend Section 2 title from 'Safety-Stack Coordination' to 'Safety-Stack Coordination and Cross-Component Parity'
- Add PARITY-1 entry after LEASE-1 (Flaw/Why/Fix/Lesson shape)
- Update Section 2 review checklist with 1 new bullet
- Update Appendix B summary table with PARITY-1 row
- Add Appendix A changelog entry
- Update ToC entry count for Section 2 (HOOK-1, LEASE-1 -> HOOK-1, LEASE-1, PARITY-1)

Spec authority: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-18-tuxlink-arv-lease-dir-parity-design.md §3.3
Plan authority: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-18-tuxlink-arv-lease-dir-fix.md Task 8 (lines 528-633, full step-by-step including exact entry text)

Workflow:
1. Wait for PR #43 + PR #44 to merge
2. bd update <this-issue> --claim
3. new_tuxlink_worktree.py --slug parity-1-pitfalls --issue <this-issue>
4. Apply plan v2 Task 8 steps
5. Commit + push + open PR against feat/v0.0.1 (tiny diff; should review fast)
6. Close on merge

Why deferred: PR #44's fix + tests are complete and reviewable as a standalone; PARITY-1 is the docs reinforcement that complements HOOK-1 (agent behavior) and LEASE-1 (single-source-of-truth) with the script/hook code-structure rule. Decoupling avoids force-push complexity and keeps PR #44 focused.
notes: Worktree path: /home/administrator/Code/tuxlink/worktrees/bd-tuxlink-0e1-parity-1-pitfalls. Branch: bd-tuxlink-0e1/parity-1-pitfalls. Created 2026-05-18 09:55:34 UTC by oak-fjord-swallow.

value: Path C accepted 2026-05-17: skip WINE-Express walkthrough; do static-artifact mining instead under bd-tuxlink-x5p. Cameron rated the walkthrough's marginal value below the cost of either rebooting Pi onto 4k-page kernel or pivoting to Windows. Static analysis of CHM + Cameron's installed RMS Express dir + the official installer is the substitute. Findings land in docs/design/v0.0.1-ux-mockups.md.

PR #33 (bd-tuxlink-x5p/canonical-design-doc) opens 2026-05-17 with 529-line canonical UX design doc at docs/design/v0.0.1-ux-mockups.md. Synthesizes brainstorm + Express artifact static audit. Queues AMD-1..AMD-10 follow-up PRs in §9. Closes tuxlink-x5p via deliverable when merged.
